parsedContent.title: School Bursar & Accounting — Practice Exam
parsedContent.description: 50 questions · Multiple choice · Click 'Show answer' to reveal the correct option and explanation

questions.text: A school's cash book shows a bank balance of 2,500,000 FRW. Bank charges of 45,000 FRW appear on the bank statement but have not yet been recorded in the cash book. What is the adjusted cash book balance?
A school issued cheques totalling 800,000 FRW, but only 600,000 FRW had been presented to the bank by year end. The bank statement balance is 3,200,000 FRW. What is the cash book balance?
Which of the following would cause the bank statement balance to be HIGHER than the cash book balance?

options.text: Bank charges not in cash book

questions.text: A school received 350,000 FRW from a parent by direct bank transfer. This has not been entered in the cash book. To update the cash book, the entry is:

A school cashier started the week with a petty cash float of 200,000 FRW. Expenditures during the week were: stationery 35,000 FRW, cleaning materials 48,000 FRW, postage 12,000 FRW. How much must be reimbursed at the end of the week?
Interest earned of 18,000 FRW credited by the bank does not appear in the school's cash book. This causes:

options.text: Cash book balance > bank statement balance
Bank statement balance > cash book balance
Cash book to show a debit balance

questions.text: A cheque of 120,000 FRW was deposited by the school and entered in the cash book, but was later dishonoured. The correction entry in the cash book is:
A standing order payment of 75,000 FRW appears on the bank statement but not in the cash book. To reconcile, the cash book balance should be:

The following items are identified during bank reconciliation: unpresented cheques 180,000 FRW; outstanding deposits 250,000 FRW. The bank statement shows 4,600,000 FRW. What is the correct cash book balance?
Which document must a school prepare to explain the difference between the cash book balance and the bank statement balance?

A school has maximum consumption of 5,000 units/week and minimum consumption of 3,000 units/week. The re-order period is 3–5 weeks and the re-order quantity is 24,000 units. What is the maximum stock level?
Annual demand for school exercise books is 18,000 units, ordering cost is $40 per order, and holding cost is $0.50 per unit per year. Calculate the Economic Order Quantity (EOQ).
A school store has opening stock of 5,000 units, purchases of 30,000 units, and closing stock of 8,000 units. What is the cost of materials consumed?
Under the FIFO (First-In, First-Out) method of inventory valuation, during a period of rising prices, the closing stock value will be:
A school store keeper issued 200 units at $5 each and 300 units at $6 each. Opening stock was 600 units at $4. What is the value of closing stock of 100 units using FIFO?
The re-order level is calculated as:
A school's minimum stock level is 4,000 units, re-order level is 10,000 units, and minimum consumption is 1,500 units/week. What is the minimum re-order period?
Materials costing 450,000 FRW were returned to the supplier after purchase. The correct journal entry is:

options.text: Dr Purchases A/C, Cr Returns Outwards A/C
Dr Returns Outwards A/C, Cr Purchases A/C
Dr Creditors A/C, Cr Returns Outwards A/C
Dr Returns Outwards A/C, Cr Creditors A/C

questions.text: Which inventory control system requires a physical count of stock at regular intervals?
The average stock level formula is:
A school purchased laboratory equipment costing $80,000 with a useful life of 10 years and no salvage value. Using the straight-line method, what is the annual depreciation?
A school van was purchased for 6,000,000 FRW. It is depreciated at 25% per annum on the reducing balance method. What is the depreciation charge in Year 2?
The cost of painting a school building is classified as:
Accumulated depreciation is shown in the balance sheet as:

options.text: A deduction from the related fixed asset

questions.text: A school bought furniture for 1,200,000 FRW with a salvage value of 120,000 FRW and useful life of 6 years. Calculate the rate of depreciation under the straight-line method.
Which accounting concept requires that the same depreciation method be applied consistently from year to year?
A school machine cost $120,000. Tax depreciation is 40% reducing balance. Tax rate is 30%. Calculate the tax depreciation shield in Year 1.
When a fixed asset is sold at a price higher than its net book value, the difference is recorded as:
Which of the following is a capital expenditure?

A school project costs $60,000 and is depreciated over 5 years (straight line, no residual). Average annual earnings before depreciation and tax are $20,000. Tax rate is 40%. What is the Accounting Rate of Return?
When a debtor's account is confirmed as irrecoverable, the entry to write off the bad debt is:

options.text: Dr Bad Debts A/C, Cr Debtors A/C
Dr Debtors A/C, Cr Bad Debts A/C
Dr Provision for Bad Debts A/C, Cr Profit & Loss A/C
Dr Profit & Loss A/C, Cr Debtors A/C

questions.text: A school increases its provision for bad debts from 80,000 FRW to 110,000 FRW. The accounting entry for the increase is:

options.text: Dr Profit & Loss A/C 30,000, Cr Provision for Bad Debts A/C 30,000
Dr Provision for Bad Debts A/C 30,000, Cr Profit & Loss A/C 30,000
Dr Bad Debts A/C 30,000, Cr Debtors A/C 30,000
Dr Debtors A/C 30,000, Cr Provision for Bad Debts A/C 30,000

questions.text: A previously written-off debt of 45,000 FRW is unexpectedly recovered. The correct entries are:

options.text: Dr Cash A/C, Cr Bad Debts Recovered A/C
Dr Bad Debts Recovered A/C, Cr Cash A/C
Dr Provision for Bad Debts, Cr Cash A/C
Dr Debtors A/C, Cr Profit & Loss A/C

questions.text: Opening debtors: 2,200,000 FRW. Credit sales during the year: 8,500,000 FRW. Cash received from debtors: 7,800,000 FRW. Bad debts written off: 150,000 FRW. Calculate closing debtors.
The provision for doubtful debts appears in the balance sheet as:

options.text: An item in the income and expenditure account

questions.text: Which of the following is an example of an error of principle?

options.text: Recording cash sales in the wrong column
Posting a capital purchase to repairs expense account

questions.text: A suspense account is opened because:

options.text: The trial balance totals are equal
The trial balance totals do not agree

questions.text: Sales of 560,000 FRW were recorded as 650,000 FRW in the sales day book. This is an error of:
Which of the following errors will be revealed by a trial balance?

options.text: A transaction completely omitted from the books
A transaction entered on the wrong side of both accounts
A single entry — a debit with no corresponding credit
An entry made to the correct side of the wrong account

questions.text: Which concept requires that anticipated losses are recognised immediately while anticipated profits are not recognised until realised?
A school receives subscription income. Subscriptions received in advance at year end are treated as:
Opening creditors: 1,800,000 FRW. Credit purchases: 9,200,000 FRW. Cash paid to creditors: 8,500,000 FRW. Returns outwards: 300,000 FRW. Calculate closing creditors.
Which financial statement shows an organisation's financial position at a specific date?
A school has total assets of 45,000,000 FRW and total liabilities of 18,000,000 FRW. What is the capital/net worth of the school?
Under the accruals concept, expenses paid in advance at year end are shown in the balance sheet as:
A school's income and expenditure account for the year shows total income of 12,400,000 FRW and total expenditure of 10,900,000 FRW. The surplus for the year is:
The receipts and payments account of a non-profit school is similar to a:

A school purchases goods on credit for 3,600,000 FRW. A trade discount of 10% is offered and a cash discount of 5% is available if paid within 30 days. If the school pays within 30 days, the amount paid is:
Which of the following is NOT a book of prime (original) entry?
A school's gross profit is 8,000,000 FRW, net profit is 5,200,000 FRW, and cost of sales is 12,000,000 FRW. Calculate the gross profit margin.
